fact: April 29 is exactly 119 days into the year - that's almost exactly one-third of the way through!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for baby animals to be born because the weather is getting warmer and food is becoming plentiful again.
Cherry blossom petals are often falling like pink snow in many parts of Japan and Korea right around this date, creating magical carpets on the ground.
The sun is rising about 2 minutes earlier each day in most places, so mornings are getting noticeably brighter by the end of April.
People born on April 29 are Taurus bulls - they're stubborn but also super loyal, just like the animal they're named after!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, leaves are typically turning brilliant orange and red as autumn kicks into high gear.
Dandelions are usually at their peak fluffiness right now - those white seed heads are perfect for making wishes and blowing seeds everywhere.
This date falls almost exactly halfway between the spring equinox and summer solstice in the north, making it a perfect balance point of the season.
Migrating butterflies like monarchs are often spotted in huge groups around this time as they follow warm weather patterns northward.
In many cold countries, this is typically when the last patches of snow finally melt away, revealing all the weird stuff that got buried during winter.
Tree frogs usually start their loudest singing competitions around this date because it's mating season - they can be so noisy they wake up entire neighborhoods!
